visual_dna.age_range: ~20-25 — likely the youngest of the twelve, possibly a teenager when called
visual_dna.skin_tone: tanned but slightly lighter than Peter — possibly less time in the sun due to age
visual_dna.beard_style: young man's beard — less full than the older disciples
visual_dna.eye_color: dark brown — observant, takes everything in, deeply feeling
visual_dna.height_build: lean, younger build — not yet the physical weight of Peter or James
distinguishing_marks: the youngest energy in the group — quick, observant
runs to the tomb and outruns Peter (John 20) — his youth shows in his body
present at everything important — Transfiguration, Garden of Gethsemane, the cross itself
the only male disciple recorded at the crucifixion in all four gospels

wardrobe_by_arc.fishing_call: working clothes with James — they were mending nets when Jesus called
wardrobe_by_arc.last_supper: close to Jesus at the table — the one 'whom Jesus loved' reclining next to him
wardrobe_by_arc.at_the_cross: present when others fled — witness to everything

voice_profile.register: younger tenor — he has not yet grown into the full voice he will have
voice_profile.pace: observant, thoughtful — asks deeper questions than most of the disciples
voice_profile.accent_note: Galilean — same regional accent as James and Peter
speech_mannerisms: tends to ask the quiet question others are afraid to ask
with James at Luke 9:54 — willing to suggest calling fire, but less loud about it
the one who reports to Jesus that they stopped someone casting out demons 'because he doesn't follow us' — a legalism Jesus corrects
voice_profile.notes: John's emotional intelligence sets him apart from the other disciples. He observes more than he speaks. That quality will make him the mystic writer of the fourth gospel.

emotional_arc.opening: Young fisherman with his father and brother — called the same day as Peter.
emotional_arc.midpoint: Inner circle — the relationship with Jesus deepens beyond what the others have access to.
emotional_arc.climax: At the cross — standing with the women, with Mary, when all other male disciples except possibly others are gone.
emotional_arc.resolution: The beloved disciple — his arc in Luke prepares but does not complete. He carries what he witnessed.

evangelical_baptist.notes: The Beloved Disciple — his later writings (Gospel of John, Revelation) are deeply embedded in evangelical theology
evangelical_baptist.marketing_angle: He was there for everything. And he never left.

catholic.notes: St. John the Evangelist — his gospel and epistles are central to Catholic mystical tradition
catholic.marketing_angle: The one Jesus trusted with his mother. The mystic who wrote 'God is love.'

black_church.marketing_angle: He stayed at the cross when others ran. Witness matters.

hispanic_christian.notes: The beloved disciple — his closeness to Jesus resonates
hispanic_christian.marketing_angle: El discípulo amado — el que se quedó

secular_curious.notes: The youngest one who became the deepest thinker — an intriguing arc
secular_curious.marketing_angle: The youngest disciple became the oldest apostle. And never stopped writing.

youth_gen_z.notes: Possibly a teenager when called — the youngest, the most emotionally perceptive
youth_gen_z.marketing_angle: He might have been seventeen when Jesus called him. He's the one who stayed at the cross.

jewish_american.marketing_angle: A young Galilean fisherman — the world of 1st century Jewish fishing community

women.notes: He was trusted with Mary at the cross — 'Woman, here is your son.' His closeness to women disciples is distinctive
women.marketing_angle: He was trusted to care for Mary. He was with the women at the cross. He sees what the others miss.

men.notes: The young man who became the one who loved most deeply — different kind of strength
men.marketing_angle: The youngest one. The one who ran fastest to the tomb. The one who never stopped believing.

audience_targeting.modern_parallel: The young observer who feels everything deeply and becomes the one who loves most
audience_targeting.modern_parallel_detail: He was likely a teenager when called. He is the one who leans on Jesus, who stays at the cross, who runs first to the tomb. His strength is not Peter's strength — it is a different kind.

ai_generation.consistency_seed_prompt: 1st century Jewish young Galilean fisherman man age 20-25, tanned skin, dark brown hair, young man's beard, lean youthful build, simple traveling tunic, observant and thoughtful expression, Galilean hillside or lake setting, cinematic photoreal, warm natural light, 8k
ai_generation.negative_prompt: old man, European features, cartoonish, fine robes, halo, long flowing robes
ai_generation.midjourney_style_params: cinematic photoreal, warm natural daylight, youthful energy, Galilean world
ai_generation.firefly_prompt: Photorealistic portrait of a young 1st century Jewish man, age 20-25, youthful lean build, tanned olive skin, dark hair, light beard, wearing simple traveling tunic and robe, thoughtful observant expression, Galilean landscape, warm natural light, cinematic framing
reference_films: The Chosen TV series — for young disciple world-building
ai_generation.color_grading_notes: Same warm Galilean palette as James — but slightly lighter and more youthful.

production_notes: Cast John visibly younger than the others — the age contrast with Peter matters. His closeness to Jesus should be visible in blocking and positioning.
